title: Purely Sweet Algerian Apricot Tagine
description: A sweet and juicy Algerian dish featuring caramelized apricots and ginger paste, perfect for a sick day, and is Jain-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ginger paste and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the apricots, Algerian spice blend, honey, water, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the apricots are tender.
Remove the lid and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the apricots are caramelized.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
